name: Stephanie's Chocolate Raspberry Tart
recipeInstructions:
CRUST:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Put flour, sugar, and cocoa in food processor and process with metal blade until combined. Add the butter, cut into cubes, and process until crumbly. Press the mixture into a 10-12-inch removable bottom tart pan. Bake for 10-12 minutes. Remove from oven and cool.
RASPBERRY FILLING:
Place unflavored gelatin in a small cup. Add a small amount of berry juice and soften. Heat until dissolved (if using a microwave, heat about 1 1/2 to 2 minutes). Puree the raspberries in processor with metal blade. If you don't have a processor, mash the berries through a sieve. Prefer a seed-free filling? Push the purée through the sieve, discarding the seeds. Add a small amount of sugar to unsweetened berries to taste. Add gelatin mixture and place in freezer until it begins to thicken. Spread mixture over cooled tart shell.
CHOCOLATE FILLING:
Melt semi-sweet chocolate in double boiler and cool (or microwave for 3 minutes just below full power, stirring frequently). Beat butter and sugar together until light and fluffy. Add cooled melted chocolate to butter and sugar mixture and beat 10 minutes or until sugar is dissolved. Add egg substitute slowly, one egg at a time, beating 5 minutes after each egg. Spread this mixture smoothly over raspberry filling.
DESIGN:
Melt semi-sweet chocolate with butter in a small saucepan, stirring continuously. Pour mixture into pastry bag. Using #1 tube tip, start at the center of the tart and make a spiral pattern, squeezing gently. While chocolate is still wet, use a sharp knife to draw a line from the center to the edge of the tart. Repeat this 15 times, dividing the tart into servings.
If you don't have a pastry tube, take a white envelope and clip off one corner just at the edge. Add the chocolate and gently squeeze out the chocolate.
Serves 14-16.
